channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Sonic Frontiers Definitive Edition Features Revealed
item.content:encoded:
A reliable insider has revealed what’s included (features) in Sonic Frontiers Definitive Edition for the Switch 2.

Last week, players were informed about the release date and price of Sonic Frontiers Definitive Edition on the Switch 2; however, there was one thing that left players confused: what they would be receiving in this new edition of the game. Now, all the details have come to light.

Sonic Frontiers Definitive Edition Brings All DLC, Gameplay Improvements, and Enhanced Visuals to the Table

A new report by billbil-kun on Dealabs reveals all the features included in Sonic Frontiers Definitive Edition. Firstly, players can expect all DLC, updates, and improvements that developers have made to the game since its release. On top of that, players will experience enhanced performance and fidelity on the Switch 2, along with reduced load times, making it a more technically optimized version than the one released on the original Switch.

The developers have yet to officially announce this version for the Switch 2, but players pretty much know what to expect. There’s also a physical release planned for Nintendo’s new console, but it is unclear whether it will come to other platforms. As revealed in the previous report, Sonic Frontiers Definitive Edition will release for the Switch 2 on Sonic’s birthday, June 23, 2026, and will cost $49.99 in the United States.

In other news, Sega’s 2021 project, Super Game, has been canceled due to the poor performance of free-to-play titles. Additionally, Sonic the Hedgehog 4 director Jeff Fowler confirmed filming has wrapped and teased the first look at Metal Sonic. What are your thoughts on the features of Sonic Frontiers Definitive Edition? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Destiny 2 players hosting a ‘Slam the Server’ Party. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Insider Gaming Showcase Participant Hounded Signs Publisher Deal
item.content:encoded:
The Insider Gaming Showcase airs on Thursday, May 28, at 3pm, and already, there is major news to announce for one of the participants.

Imprnt Studios, the developers behind showcase participant Hounded, have reached an agreement with UK publisher Silver Lining Interactive.

“We’re delighted to partner with the exceptional team at IMPRNT STUDIOS on their new game Hounded,” Luke Keighran, Founder and Managing Director at Silver Lining Interactive, said. “The game delivers a compelling emotional story, a rich fantasy world, and a wonderfully vibrant lead character – it is exactly the kind of project we love to champion!”

Imprnt Studios CEO and co-founder Calvin Williams said that at the start of their talks, Silver Lining “understood the emotional core and creative ambitions behind the game.”

Jack Walker, CCO and co-founder of Imprnt Studios, added: “We can’t wait to share more with players during the Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28.”

Hounded is a third-person narrative adventure where you play as a Border Collie named Duke who is on the search for his missing master. As you play, you are guided by scent, instinct, and a mysterious force known as The Tide.

“Navigate a dangerous world filled with secrets, enemies, and unlikely allies in a desperate journey to reunite with the person he loves most,” Imprnt Studios says.

As mentioned, Hounded will be part of the Insider Gaming Showcase, giving players a look at the environment of the game and Duke. You’ll also learn more details on the game in our exclusive sit-down interview with game creator Calvin Williams, airing immediately following the show.

Watch the Insider Gaming Showcase exclusively on Insider Gaming’s YouTube Channel, website, and in the Insider Gaming App on May 28 at 3pm ET/12pm PT/8pm BST. The showcase features more than 50 games, including 12 world premieres, that are looking for funding or publisher support.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Destiny 2 Players Aiming to ‘Slam Servers’ With Big Party on June 9
item.content:encoded:
The Destiny conversation is only getting louder, as players are continuing to protest about the series apparently coming to a standstill. The newest twist is that Destiny 2 players plan to overload the servers on June 9 as an act of defiance.

If you’ve not been keeping up with the Destiny developments, oh boy. It’s been a wild month as Bungie announced Destiny 2 was getting no more updates. Not only that, but the news on Bungie making Destiny 3 after Destiny 2 isn’t good either.

With a petition ongoing and accruing serious signatures by the minute, the community has come up with another initiative.

Destiny 2 Slam The Server Party Invite for June 9

Community uproar has been on full display with the Destiny petition. Find out more about how to sign the ‘Save Destiny’ petition and all details right here, for more information.

Now, a Reddit post has gained traction and started to appear across all social media platforms that mention Destiny 2.

‘Everyone – Log in on June 9th’ is the simple message on the Destiny subreddit.

User w1nds0r has a plan:

We need to at least smash Marathon’s all time high to show them they made the wrong decision.

Heresy was amazing, the edge of fate story was interesting. They let this happen by doubling down on bad system changes and ignoring player feedback.

I feel like everyone should make a decent effort to log in on June 9th to show them this is a franchise worth continuing to invest in. If you can, try to get your friend and clan mates to all log in when the last update drops, it’s our last chance to send a message the franchise is still valuable. That’s all really.

Take care!’

The post has thousands of upvotes and over 1K comments on the idea. Now, yes, Reddit represents barely a portion of a game’s worldwide playerbase.

But it only takes one message or one movement to make a difference.

But the message is also being spread across other forms, including X.

See this post from Kaine Richardson.

Even if hundreds of thousands of players log in on June 9, it doesn’t mean Bungie or PlayStation will even blink an eye. All you can do is try, so we’ll see what happens come June 9.

In other news, please help support us by watching the free Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28! There will be 55 games, including 12 World Premieres, new trailers, and more.

If you have grievances or want to vent about the whole Destiny saga, check out the Insider Gaming Discord to let your feelings be known!

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton
